title: fresh and dense Norwegian bellflower-root-doraji knekkeboller
description: Festive Norwegian bellflower-root-doraji knekkeboller are dense and tender, perfect for a cold winter's night. This unique dish combines the earthy flavor of bellflower root with the richness of eggs and cream, all wrapped up in a delicate pastry crust.

ingredients.name: all-purpose gluten-free flout
ingredients.notes: use a high-quality gluten-free flour blend

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine bellflower root, gluten-free flour, eggs, heavy cream, granulated sugar, and salt. Mix until a sticky dough forms.
Turn dough out onto a floured surface and knead for 5 minutes, until smooth and elastic.
Divide dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each into a ball.
Dip each ball into melted butter to coat, then roll in puff pastry to coat evenly.
Place coated dough balls onto a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving about 1 inch of space between each knekkeboller.
Brush tops with melted butter and sprinkle with granulated sugar.
Bake for 25 minutes, or until pastry is golden brown and dough is cooked through.
Remove from oven and let cool for 5 minutes before serving.
Garnish with chopped parsley and serve warm.
Serve with your favorite sides, such as lingonberry jam or pickled vegetables.
Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
Reheat in the oven or microwave before serving.
Freeze knekkeboller on a baking sheet, then transfer to a freezer-safe bag or container for up to 2 months.
To reheat frozen knekkeboller, bake in a preheated oven at 37520 (19020C) for 15-20 minutes, or until warmed through.
